SCHEDULE: Retiring Allowance

CHAPTER 1.11
RETIRING ALLOWANCES (LEGISLATIVE
SERVICE) (INCREASE) ORDINANCE
(Ordinance 29 of 2004)
AN ORDINANCE TO INCREASE THE RATES OF RETIRING ALLOWANCE PAID TO
RETIRED LEGISLATORS AND FOR CONNECTED PURPOSES.
Commencement
[3 January 2005]
Short title
1. This Ordinance may be cited as the Retiring Allowances (Legislative
Service) (Increase) Ordinance.
Interpretation
2. In this Ordinance—
“award” means any money payable out of the Consolidated Fund to any person
pursuant to the provisions of the Retiring Allowances (Legislative
Service) Ordinance;
© Crown Copyright 2014
Copying/unauthorised distribution strictly prohibited.

Revision Date: 31 Dec 2014
“retiring allowance” means a retiring allowance payable pursuant to section 3 of
the Retiring Allowances (Legislative Service) Ordinance.
Increase of retiring allowances
3. (1) A person who is in receipt of, or eligible for, a retiring allowance by
virtue of the Retiring Allowances (Legislative Service) Ordinance shall, if his
effective date of qualification under the Ordinance for the receipt of the retiring
allowance is as provided in the Schedule, be entitled to the retiring allowance at
the increased rate of his entitlement as stipulated in the Schedule.
(2) For the purpose of subsection (1) and the Schedule, a person’s
effective date of qualification for the receipt of a retiring allowance is the date
from which he is awarded or entitled to a retiring allowance under the Retiring
Allowance (Legislative Service) Ordinance.
(3) The increase in retiring allowances provided in the Schedule shall be
payable with effect from 1st April 2004.
Powers to amend Schedule
4. (1) The Governor may, by Order published in the Gazette, amend the
Schedule.
(2) No amendment shall be made under subsection (1) unless the draft
Order thereof has been laid before the House of Assembly and approved by a
resolution of the House.
............................
SCHEDULE Retiring Allowance
Section 3(1)
The effective date of qualification for receipt of the increase in retiring allowance is
prior to 1995.
OFFICE
NEW ALLOWANCE
PER ANNUM
$

(b) Deputy Chief Minister and Minister …………………. 22,400
(c) Speaker ……………………………………………….. 10,677
(d) Leader of the Opposition ……………………...……… 8,800
(e) Elected Member ……………………….................... 8,000
(f) Nominated Member …………………………............... 5,280
